description: A host is observed using an anomalous or unexpected HTTP user agent. This could indicate unauthorized or malicious activity on the network.
significance: The use of an anomalous HTTP user agent can indicate unauthorized access or malicious activity, as adversaries might employ user agents that are either non-standard or atypical for the given host.
anomalous_behavior: :original_entity: used :item:, a user agent they and similar entities had not used during baselining
baseline_behavior: :entity: used :entity_training_items_combined: for HTTP connections during baselining

description: A host established an SSH connection to an unexpected subnet. This may indicate lateral movement or unauthorized access attempts.
significance: Anomalous SSH connections to unexpected subnets can indicate lateral movement by adversaries or unauthorized access to sensitive resources.
anomalous_behavior: :original_entity: established an SSH connection to subnet :item, a destination they and similar entities had not connected to during baselining
baseline_behavior: :entity: established SSH connections to these :entity_training_items_combined: during baselining

description: A host established an SSH connection using an unexpected or anomalous SSH client. This could indicate the use of malicious tools or unauthorized access.
significance: The use of an anomalous SSH client can indicate malicious activity, as adversaries might employ SSH clients that are either non-standard or atypical for the given host.
anomalous_behavior: :original_entity: used SSH client :item:, an SSH client they and similar entities had not used during baselining

description: A host communicated with another internal subnet over an anomalous or unexpected network service, indicating potential lateral movement or misconfiguration.
significance: Anomalous services between internal subnets can indicate lateral movement by adversaries or unauthorized access to resources.
anomalous_behavior: :original_entity: used :item:, a service they and similar entities had not used during baselining
baseline_behavior: :entity: communicated with internal subnet using :entity_training_items_combined: during baselining

description: An internet-facing server found in connections with an unexpected network service. This may indicate exploitation or misconfiguration.
significance: Internet-facing servers can be exploited by adversaries to gain unauthorized access or disrupt operations.
anomalous_behavior: :original_entity: used :item:, a service they and similar entities had not used during baselining

description: A host established an RDP connection to an unexpected subnet using an unusual SSL/TLS fingerprint.
significance: Anomalous RDP connections to unexpected destinations can indicate lateral movement by adversaries, unauthorized remote access, or compromise of legitimate credentials for unauthorized system access.
anomalous_behavior: :original_entity: used RDP or Port 3389/tcp to connect to :item: for at least 15 seconds, a subnet to which it had unexpectedly connected using an unusual SSL/TLS fingerprint

description: A host accessed an unexpected administrative network share (ADMIN$, IPC$, SYSVOL, NETLOGON).
significance: Anomalous administrative share access can indicate lateral movement by adversaries or unauthorized access to sensitive administrative resources and network infrastructure.
anomalous_behavior: :original_entity: unexpectedly accessed :item:, an administrative network share (ADMIN$, IPC$, SYSVOL, NETLOGON) in a way that deviated from its baseline

description: A host downloaded an unexpected executable file from an external or internal source.
significance: Anomalous executable downloads can indicate malicious tool transfer, malware distribution, or unauthorized software installation that could be used for persistence or lateral movement.
anomalous_behavior: :original_entity: transferred :item:, an executable file they and similar entities had not used during baselining
